title: LRI Database Curation for Benchmarking

code: # Cytokine to Gene Mapping - Only for CytoSig list cytokine_to_gene_mapping <- list(   "INHBA" = "INHBA",   "BDNF" = "BDNF",    "BMP2" = "BMP2",   "BMP4" = "BMP4",   "BMP6" = "BMP6",    "BMP7" = "BMP7",   "CCL2" = "CCL2",   "CXCL12" = "CXCL12",   "EGF" = "EGF",   "EPO" = "EPO",   "FGF2" = "FGF2",   "GDF11" = "GDF11",    "HGF" = "HGF",   "HMGB1" = "HMGB1",   "IFNG" = "IFNG",   "IGF1" = "IGF1",   "IL10" = "IL10",   "IL13" = "IL13",   "IL15" = "IL15",    "IL17A" = "IL17A",   "IL18" = "IL18",   "IL1A" = "IL1A",   "IL1B" = "IL1B",    "IL2" = "IL2",   "IL21" = "IL21",   "IL22" = "IL22",   "IL3" = "IL3",   "IL33" = "IL33",   "IL4" = "IL4",   "IL6" = "IL6",   "INS" = "INS",   "LIF" = "LIF",    "LTA" = "LTA",   "NRG1" = "NRG1",   "OSM" = "OSM",   "PDGFB" = "PDGFB",   "PDGFD" = "PDGFD",   "TGFA" = "TGFA",   "TGFB1" = "TGFB1",   "TGFB3" = "TGFB3",   "TSLP" = "TSLP",   "VEGFA" = "VEGFA",    "WNT3A" = "WNT3A",   "WNT5A" = "WNT5A",      # Single gene conversions (name changes)   "CD40L" = "CD40LG",   "GCSF" = "CSF3",    "GMCSF" = "CSF2",   "IFN1" = "IFNA1",   "IFNL" = "IFNL1",   "MCSF" = "CSF1",   "TNFA" = "TNF",   "TRAIL" = "TNFSF10",   "TWEAK" = "TNFSF12",   "PGE2" = "PTGS2",   # Multi-subunit cytokines (only ones in CytoSig list)   "IL12" = c("IL12A", "IL12B"),   "IL27" = c("IL27A", "EBI3"),    "IL36" = c("IL36A", "IL36B", "IL36G") )

what: The following is a helper function that reduces the LRI dbs down to CytoSig features
title: LRI Database Curation for Benchmarking
text: Remove instances feature references prevent bias gene expression
code: # Single function to create CytoSig only features across databases create_cytokine_dbs <- function(dbs_all, cytokine_mapping) {   # For each database   lapply(dbs_all, function(db) {     # For each cytokine in mapping     cytokine_features <- lapply(names(cytokine_mapping), function(cytokine) {       genes <- cytokine_mapping[[cytokine]]              # Get all receptors for these genes (ignore missing genes)       all_receptors <- unlist(db[genes[genes %in% names(db)]])       unique_receptors <- unique(all_receptors[!is.na(all_receptors)])              # Remove only exact cytokine name matches (keep subunit interactions)       unique_receptors[unique_receptors != cytokine]     })          # Name the list and remove empty features     names(cytokine_features) <- names(cytokine_mapping)     cytokine_features[lengths(cytokine_features) > 0]   }) }

title: LRI Database Curation for Benchmarking
code: dbs_cytosig <- create_cytokine_dbs(dbs_all, cytokine_to_gene_mapping)

what: Set up the design matrix for demo data
title: cytokineFindeR-introduction
code: # Input treatment: difference between week 0 and week 6 # input obs_id if paired data (in this case we do have paired data), GSM IDs different for same sample IDs due to different time points  design <- create_design(treatment, obs_id, eset) #> Within-block correlation: 0.209764916604386 design_mat <- design$design dupcor <- design$dupcor

title: cytokineFindeR-introduction
code: preprocess_list <- preprocess_eset(eset, dbs) eset_f <- preprocess_list$eset_f dbs_f <- preprocess_list$dbs_f

what: Call the databases, list out the methods to benchmark, run cytokinefinder()
title: cytokineFindeR-introduction
text: result return nested list benchmark results
code: methods <- c("gsva_limma",              "pca_limma")  results <- run_lri_methods(eset_f,                            design_mat,                            dbs_f,                            methods,                            treatment = treatment,                            obs_id = obs_id,                            correlation = dupcor$consensus                           )

what: To reconstruct the list of benchmarks into a tibble:
title: cytokineFindeR-introduction
text: benchlist_to_tbl rank cytokine interest based name assignment provided golimumab_treatment, case, benchmark results named TNF, function output ranking TNF across method+db combinations.
code: golimumab_treatment <- list(TNF = list(benchmarks = results)) golimumab_tibble <- benchlist_to_tbl(results_list = golimumab_treatment,                                      study_type = "treatment") golimumab_tibble

code: @Article{,   title = {cytokineFindeR: an R-package for benchmarking methods and databases for identifying cytokines},   author = {Jeffrey Tang and Harnoor Dhillon and Asees Singh and Amrit Singh},   journal = {bioRxiv},   year = {2025},   url = {https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2025.09.30.679635v1},   note = {R package version 0.99.0}, }

title: Measure and rank ligands of interest (cytokines) across different methods and databases
text: cytokineFindeR R package measuring ranking cytokines signaling ligands across multiple statistical methods ligand-receptor interaction (LRI) databases. uses receptor gene sets derived curated LRI databases proxies upstream ligand activity - enabling transcriptomic inference cytokine signaling without requiring direct cytokine measurements. package supports: 4 statistical methods linear models inferring cytokine activity based LRI databases custom implementation CytoSig, ridge regression model trained 20,591 transcriptomic profiles Multiple curated LRI databases (e.g., BaderLab, LIANA+, CellChatv2, Omnipath) Rank-based ensemble scoring combining LRI-based methods CytoSig Paired unpaired experimental designs duplicate correlation support Parallel execution via future future.apply

title: Measure and rank ligands of interest (cytokines) across different methods and databases
text: Given gene expression matrix (bulk RNA-seq microarray) sample group labels, cytokineFindeR infers cytokines ligands differentially active conditions testing whether associated receptor gene sets coordinately - -regulated. Results multiple methods databases aggregated produce robust, consensus ranking.

title: Measure and rank ligands of interest (cytokines) across different methods and databases
code: if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install("cytokineFindeR")

title: Measure and rank ligands of interest (cytokines) across different methods and databases
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("CompBio-Lab/cytokineFinder", build_vignettes = TRUE)

title: Measure and rank ligands of interest (cytokines) across different methods and databases
code: library(cytokineFindeR)  # Load the included demo dataset (golimumab anti-TNF treatment, GSE92415) data(golimumab) data(dbs_cytosig)  eset      <- golimumab$qc_eset treatment <- golimumab$cond obs_id    <- golimumab$obs_id   # paired sample IDs  # Select databases (3 of the available curated LRI databases) dbs <- dbs_cytosig[names(dbs_cytosig) %in% c("baderlab", "lianaplus", "cellchat")]  # Create design matrix (handles paired and unpaired designs) design     <- create_design(treatment, obs_id, eset) design_mat <- design$design dupcor     <- design$dupcor  # Preprocess: filter databases and expression set for compatible features preprocess <- preprocess_eset(eset, dbs) eset_f     <- preprocess$eset_f dbs_f      <- preprocess$dbs_f  # Run benchmarking across methods in parallel methods <- c("gsva_limma", "pca_limma")  results <- run_lri_methods(   eset        = eset_f,   design      = design_mat,   dbs         = dbs_f,   methods     = methods,   treatment   = treatment,   obs_id      = obs_id,   correlation = dupcor$consensus )  # Flatten results into a tidy tibble golimumab_treatment <- list(TNF = list(benchmarks = results)) tbl <- benchlist_to_tbl(results_list = golimumab_treatment,                          study_type   = "treatment") head(tbl)

ensemble with CytoSig"}} study_data <- list(qc_eset = eset, cond = treatment, obs_id = obs_id) study_data <- run_cytokinefinder( study_data = study_data, databases = dbs_f, methods = c("gsva_limma", "pca_limma", "cfgsea") )

title: Measure and rank ligands of interest (cytokines) across different methods and databases
text: cytokineFindeR implements seven approaches inferring ligand activity receptor gene sets: Paired experimental designs (pre/post matched samples) supported linear model methods via obs_id duplicate correlation parameters limma::duplicateCorrelation.

title: Measure and rank ligands of interest (cytokines) across different methods and databases
text: package ships four pre-curated dataset objects: database object named list lists, outer keys database names (e.g., "baderlab", "lianaplus", "cellchat") inner keys ligand names mapped vectors receptor gene symbols. Custom databases can saved using:
code: create_db_space(my_db_list, filePath = "data/my_lri_db.rda")

title: Measure and rank ligands of interest (cytokines) across different methods and databases
text: golimumab dataset pre-processed subset GSE92415 GEO. contains bulk RNA-seq data ulcerative colitis patients treated golimumab (anti-TNF biologic), comparing gene expression Week 0 vs. Week 6. provided named list three elements: qc_eset - normalized gene expression matrix (genes × samples) cond - treatment condition vector ("week0" / "week6") obs_id - subject IDs paired sample matching

what: Create ensemble results combining LRI-based and CytoSig methods — create_ensemble_results
title: Create ensemble results combining LRI-based and CytoSig methods — create_ensemble_results
text: function combines results LRI-based methods CytoSig results using rank-based ensemble approach. performs inner join LRI CytoSig results computes ensemble rankings based p-values.

title: Create ensemble results combining LRI-based and CytoSig methods — create_ensemble_results
code: create_ensemble_results(   master_tbl,   ensemble_method = "mean_rank",   pval_col_lri = "pval",   pval_col_cytosig = "pval" )

title: Create ensemble results combining LRI-based and CytoSig methods — create_ensemble_results
text: master_tbl tibble containing benchmark results benchlist_to_tbl(), columns: study_type, cytokine, method, database, class, ligand_tables ensemble_method Character string specifying ensemble method. Currently supports "mean_rank" (default) pval_col_lri Character string specifying p-value column name LRI method results. Default "pval" pval_col_cytosig Character string specifying p-value column name CytoSig method results. Default "pval"

title: Create ensemble results combining LRI-based and CytoSig methods — create_ensemble_results
text: tibble additional ensemble columns: overlap_count: Number overlapping ligands LRI CytoSig ensemble_table: Combined LRI CytoSig results rankings ensemble_rank: Mean rank score combining LRI CytoSig rankings lri_rank: Percentile rank LRI method (100 = best, 0 = worst) cytosig_rank: Percentile rank CytoSig method (100 = best, 0 = worst)

title: Create ensemble results combining LRI-based and CytoSig methods — create_ensemble_results
text: function: Filters LRI-based methods (class == "LRI") Joins CytoSig results (class == "CytoSig_Web") study_type cytokine Computes overlap LRI CytoSig ligand sets Creates ensemble rankings using percentile ranks (lower p-value = higher rank) Falls back CytoSig-results LRI data available Ranking system: Converts p-values percentile ranks 100 represents best (lowest p-value) 0 represents worst (highest p-value).

title: Create ensemble results combining LRI-based and CytoSig methods — create_ensemble_results
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Assuming you have benchmark results results <- run_cytokinefinder_workflow(study_data, databases, methods)  # Convert to tibble format   master_tbl <- benchlist_to_tbl(results$benchmarks, "my_study", FALSE)  # Create ensemble results ensemble_results <- create_ensemble_results(   master_tbl,    ensemble_method = "mean_rank",   pval_col_lri = "pval",   pval_col_cytosig = "pval" )  # View ensemble rankings ensemble_results %>%    select(cytokine, method, database, ensemble_rank, lri_rank, cytosig_rank) } # }

title: Golimumab Treatment Dataset (GSE92415) — golimumab
text: pre-processed subset GEO accession GSE92415 containing bulk gene expression data ulcerative colitis patients treated golimumab, anti-TNF biologic. Samples represent paired measurements Week 0 (pre-treatment) Week 6 (post-treatment) colonic tissue isolates. dataset intended use package examples vignettes.

title: Golimumab Treatment Dataset (GSE92415) — golimumab
text: named list three elements: qc_eset numeric matrix normalized gene expression values (genes x samples), rows gene symbols columns sample IDs. cond character vector indicating treatment time point sample. Values "week0" (pre-treatment) "week6" (post-treatment). obs_id character vector subject IDs used match paired samples across time points use limma::duplicateCorrelation.

code: run_cytokinefinder(study_data, databases, methods)

title: Wrapper function to run complete cytokinefinder benchmarking workflow with preprocessing — run_cytokinefinder
text: study_data Named list containing study data required elements: qc_eset: Quality-controlled expression set matrix cond: Treatment/condition vector obs_id: Sample IDs paired experiments (optional) databases List LRI databases benchmarking methods Vector method names benchmark

title: Wrapper function to run complete cytokinefinder benchmarking workflow with preprocessing — run_cytokinefinder
text: Original study_data added 'benchmarks', 'design', 'preprocessing' elements

title: Wrapper function to run complete cytokinefinder benchmarking workflow with preprocessing — run_cytokinefinder
code: # Single study example study_data <- list(   qc_eset = matrix(rnorm(1000), nrow = 100, ncol = 10),   cond = rep(c("control", "treatment"), each = 5) )  # With paired samples study_data_paired <- list(   qc_eset = matrix(rnorm(1000), nrow = 100, ncol = 10),   cond = rep(c("control", "treatment"), each = 5),   obs_id = rep(1:5, 2) )  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load databases and methods data(dbs_all)  # or however you load your databases methods <- c("gsea", "ssgsea", "cytosig_custom_ridge")  # Run workflow results <- run_cytokinefinder_workflow(study_data, dbs_all, methods)  # Access results benchmark_results <- results$benchmarks design_matrix <- results$design } # }

code: # This is the core function for running benchmarks # Basic usage: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- run_lri_methods(eset, design, dbs, methods = c("fgsea", "pca_limma")) } # }

title: cytokineFindeR 0.99.0
text: Initial Bioconductor submission. Implements seven statistical methods ligand activity inference bulk transcriptomics: gsva_limma, pca_limma, cfgsea, run_limma, gsva_plsda, pca_plsda, cytosig_custom_ridge. Includes curated ligand-receptor interaction (LRI) databases: dbs_all, dbs_subset, dbs_cytosig. Includes CytoSig beta coefficient matrix (cytosig_beta) ridge regression. Includes golimumab demo dataset (golimumab) derived GEO accession GSE92415. Supports paired unpaired experimental designs via limma::duplicateCorrelation. Parallel execution via future future.apply. Rank-based ensemble scoring combining LRI-based methods CytoSig via create_ensemble_results().
